Fun fact: Sambhu once translated an entire film script from Malayalam to English. The film was called 'Maashu'. He did not receive film credit. He has moved on. Mostly.

Relatable Energy
Staring at a blank doc like it owes me something. Every. Single. Time.
The Quill's Dilemma
First word is the hardest. Second word is also hard. Third word: surprisingly fine.
The Writing Process
Draft. Edit. Re-draft. Sleep on it. Publish. Immediately spot a typo.
WRITER. PRESENTER.
Written. Presented. Done.
I Wrote It.
Then They Said,
"Can You Present
It Too?"

At Shoolini University, I wrote scripts for promotional videos. Then somehow — I was also the one standing in front of the camera presenting it. Script to screen, one person.

Not something I lead with, but — if you ever need someone who can write the thing and show up for it, I've done that too.

(The QS World University Rankings graphic was the production team's doing. I just read the lines.)

Watch It

Shoolini University · YouTube · Written & Presented by Sambhu

Published · Economic Times
That One Time
I Almost Did
a PhD.

While assisting Prof. Bijulal M V at the Centre for Migration Policy and Inclusive Governance, MG University, I was doing what project assistants do — talking to migrant workers, attending policy meetings, liaising with government officials, digging through research.

At some point, the research got good enough that we co-wrote an opinion piece. It ended up in the Economic Times. With my name on it. And my photo. In a national newspaper.

⚠ Full Disclosure

I briefly considered a PhD. Then I remembered what a PhD involves. Moving on.

The point is: I can research. Properly. Before a word gets written.

Read the Article →
Economic Times Opinion

Co-authored with Bijulal M V · Economic Times · 2024

@ONEMORE.SKILL · #realtalks

Nergy India · @onemore.skill · Instagram Reel

On Camera. Again.
Wrote It.
Sat In The Chair.
Talked About It.

@onemore.skill is Nergy India's experiential skill learning platform — and I was in from the start. First website, social media, reels, the works. At some point, writing the scripts wasn't enough. I ended up on camera for them too.

Short reel. Real data. Comfortable chair.

Commerce Ed Script + Presenter Nergy India
Currently · Shopsy by Flipkart
S
Shopsy
by Flipkart
One Line.
One Tap.
Add to Cart.

At Shopsy, I write CRM and performance marketing copies — push notifications, banners, sale campaigns. The kind of words people read in half a second and either tap or swipe away.

These three are from the Dil Se Desi Sale — one of Shopsy's flagship sale events. The brief: patriotic, punchy, under 100 characters. The goal: get Bharat to tap.

Push Notifications CRM Copy Performance Marketing
9:41
Saturday, 15 August
SHOPSY now
Bharat shops on Dil se Desi sale 🟠⚪🟢
Get Men's Polo T-shirts from ₹99 🏃
SHOPSY 1m ago
Feel the freedom to move in every step 👟
Get Men's Sports Shoes under ₹299 on SALE
SHOPSY 2m ago
Enjoy Azaadi from MRP 🧡🤍💚
Get Dry Fruits Combos from ₹79 on SALE ⭐

Dil Se Desi Sale · Push Notifications

A Hobby. Apparently.
I Write
Subtitles
For Fun.

Perfectly normal hobby. Nothing to see here. I watched a short film, liked it, and thought — you know what this needs? Subtitles I write at 11pm for no reason whatsoever.

To be fair, the task did come from a former colleague who edited the film — so there was a reason. I just can't pretend I wouldn't have done it anyway.

P.S. — This short film is not a joke. It's about how valuable consent is. I just happen to think the best things deserve subtitles.

Subtitling For The Love Of It Short Film
youtube.com/watch · Consent · Urumbhu Productions
Consent — Full Film | Justin Jolly Venad | Vedhaa Menon | Ajinsh Vijayan | Sreejith Varma
70K VIEWS · 1.79K SUBSCRIBERS · URUMBHU PRODUCTIONS

subtitled · just because

// good_to_know_before_you_reach_out.txt
01. I show up for the brief, the deadline, and the revision round.
02. Comfortable in a team, comfortable alone. Either way, the work gets done.
03. Research first, always. Words come after the facts are in order.
04. Have written across industries — and genuinely enjoyed all of them.
05. Feedback doesn't scare me. It's just the revision round with extra context.
06. If the project is a little unusual, even better. Those tend to be the interesting ones.
response time: faster than a second draft.
